Name: Saltwater Crocodile
Key Skills: Survival (Desert)-14 / Tracking-12
Advantages: Amphibious / Doesn't Breathe (2hr) / Night Vision 5 / Peripheral Vision / Silence 3 (water)
Special Abilities: Death Roll — contested ST each round
Weakness: failure = full bite damage + submersion
Notes: Running water / Cold temperatures (<15C)
Ambush: Per roll at -2 (dusk) or -4 (night). Death roll in deep water drowns in HT rounds.

Name: Eastern Brown Snake
Advantages: Cold-Blooded / Vibration Detection 10m / Camouflage (Per -3 to spot)
Special Abilities: Venom: HT roll at 30min (HT-2 coagulopathy) / 2hr (HT-3 neurotoxin) / 4hr (HT-4 cardiac)
Weakness: Antivenin within 2 hours
Notes: Pressure immobilisation bandage adds +2 to all venom HT rolls. Multiple strikes possible.

Name: Inland Taipan
Advantages: Cold-Blooded / Enhanced Dodge 3 / Vibration Detection 10m / Camouflage (Per -3)
Special Abilities: Venom: onset 5min (HT: 1d HP) / 30min (HT-2 coagulopathy) / 1hr (HT-3 paralysis) / 2hr (HT-4 respiratory) / 3hr (HT-5 cardiac). Up to 8 bites per encounter.
Weakness: Taipan-specific antivenin
Notes: Most venomous terrestrial snake in world. Each additional bite = separate venom progression.

Name: King Brown Snake
Advantages: Cold-Blooded / Flattening display
Special Abilities: Venom: HT-2 at 1hr (myotoxin/coagulant) / HT-3 at 3hr / HT-4 at 5hr (renal) / HT-5 at 6hr (critical)
Weakness: Antivenin
Notes: Bites and holds — multiple venom doses. Multiple bites = multiple simultaneous progressions.

Name: Tiger Snake
Advantages: Cold-Blooded / Vibration Detection 8m / Flattening display
Special Abilities: Venom: HT-2 at 45min / HT-3 at 1.5hr (neurotoxin) / HT-4 at 3hr (myotoxin) / HT-5 at 5hr (renal)
Weakness: Antivenin
Notes: Primary snakebite risk in Victorian scenarios. Present along all Yarra corridor parkland.

Name: Red-Bellied Black Snake
Advantages: Cold-Blooded / Vibration Detection 8m
Special Abilities: Venom: HT-1 at 1hr (myotoxin) / HT-2 at 3hr / HT-3 at 6hr. Rarely fatal in healthy adults.
Weakness: Antivenin (cross-reactive)
Notes: Less dangerous than brown/tiger snakes but still requires antivenin. Common near waterways.

Name: Redback Spider
Advantages: Aggressive / Multiple bite capacity
Special Abilities: Venom (Latrotoxin): HT-1 at 15min (pain/cramps) / HT-2 at 1hr (systemic). Rarely fatal.
Weakness: Antivenin available
Notes: Use to establish Australia is always trying to kill you. Present in all station sheds / infrastructure.

Name: Funnel-Web Spider
Advantages: Aggressive (no flight response) / Fangs penetrate fingernails / Multiple bite capacity
Special Abilities: Venom (Atracotoxin): onset immediate / HT-2 at 15min (-2 DX) / HT-3 at 30min / HT-4 at 1hr (pulmonary) / HT-5 at 2hr (cardiac)
Weakness: Antivenin (must reach hospital)
Notes: Aggressive — will stand ground. Habitat: disturbed soil near bridge footings. Melbourne bridge scenes.

Name: Blue-Ringed Octopus
Region: Coastal rock pools — Cossack / Melbourne coast
Advantages: Camouflage (passive / no roll) / Blue ring display (Per +2 when displaying) / Aquatic
Special Abilities: Venom (Tetrodotoxin): HT at 10min / HT-2 at 20min (-3 DX) / HT-3 at 30min (respiratory paralysis) / HT-4 at 40min (full paralysis) / HT-5 at 60min (respiratory failure)
Weakness: NO antivenin exists
Notes: Treatment: artificial respiration maintained 4-6 hours. No antivenin. Can kill in 30 minutes.

Name: Stonefish
Advantages: Camouflage (perfect — Per -4 to spot) / Aquatic / Venomous dorsal spines
Special Abilities: Venom (Verrucotoxin): immediate extreme pain / HT at 30min / HT-2 at 1hr / HT-3 at 3hr (cardiac)
Weakness: Antivenin / Hot water immersion (denatures venom)
Notes: Most venomous fish in world. Looks exactly like a rock. Common in Cossack coastal scenario.

Name: Wild Boar (Feral Pig)
Advantages: Acute Smell +4 / Discrimination Smell / Night Vision 3 / Peripheral Vision
Special Abilities: Mob behaviour: 3+ pigs panicking = -2 to all player actions. 6+ in narrow terrain requires DX roll to avoid knockdown.

Name: Dingo
Advantages: Acute Hearing +3 / Acute Smell +4 / Night Vision 5 / Peripheral Vision / Pack Tactics (+2 when 3+ coordinate)
Special Abilities: Pack tactics when 3+ coordinating
Notes: Unusual behaviour near spiritual sites — depart suddenly without gradual retreat. Use as omen indicator.

Name: Wedge-Tailed Eagle
Advantages: Acute Vision +7 / Flight (Winged) / Sharp Claws and Beak
Special Abilities: Dive attack: +3 damage from height. Two warning passes before striking. Automatic attack within 10m of nest.
Notes: Unusual attentive behaviour near significant sites — perch closer than normal comfort zone.

Name: Cassowary
Advantages: Acute Hearing +2 / Acute Smell +2 / Cannot Fly / Danger Sense / Peripheral Vision / Territorial (auto-attack within 10m of nest)
Special Abilities: Jump kick: +2 damage / two simultaneous kicks at -1 each. Does not retreat when defending nest.
Weakness: Running distance / Backing away slowly
Notes: Stand tall and back away. Running triggers pursuit at Move 8. Most dangerous bird in world by injury record.

Name: Goanna (Perentie)
Advantages: Acute Smell +3 (forked tongue) / Claws / Enhanced Move 1 (sprint) / Teeth / Bacterial Load
Special Abilities: Secondary infection: HT-1 roll within 24hr of bite. Failure = -1 HT/day without antibiotics.
Weakness: Cold / Being cornered causes panic
Notes: Absent from gorge approaches = omen indicator. Present at all campsites due to food scavenging.

Name: Ghost Bat
Special Abilities: Colony disturbance (200+): zero visibility / Move halved / communication impossible / Per -4 for direction. 1d+3 minutes to settle.
Notes: Absent from deep Nullarbor chambers = marker of where conditions change.

Name: Common Wombat
Advantages: Absolute Direction (home range) / Burrowing / Tunnel Vision
Special Abilities: Collision: DX roll or 1d crush + HT roll or knockdown. Burrow hazard: Per -2 at night or step in burrow (DX or 1d ankle crush).
Notes: Absent above snowgum belt / near platform = omen. Wombat burrows are ankle-trap hazard at night.

Name: Platypus
Advantages: Aquatic
Special Abilities: Venom (Defensin peptides): immediate extreme pain / HT at 10min (hyperalgesia +2 pain penalty) / lasts up to months.
Weakness: No antivenin
Notes: Hyperalgesia: all wound pain penalties increased by modifier for duration. Not life-threatening. Extremely miserable.

Name: Bull Shark
Advantages: Enhanced Move 2 (water) / No Legs (aquatic) / Bloodscent (detects blood at 400m) / Aggressive
Special Abilities: Ambush: Per -3 in dark water. Bloodscent at 1ppm = detected at 400m. More aggressive than most shark species.
Weakness: Leaving the water
Notes: In the Yarra at 3am during bridge scenes. Not supernatural — just a bull shark in a tidal river.

Name: Magpie (Swooping)
Advantages: Aggressive (swooping season Aug–Nov)
Special Abilities: Swooping: automatic hit to head / -1 to all Per and DX while being actively swooped.
Weakness: Leaving nesting territory
Notes: Magpies near Birrarung station do NOT swoop — watching instead. Use as atmosphere indicator.

Name: Brumby Mob (Stampede)
Advantages: Enhanced Move 2 (gallop) / Flight response / Panic transmission to herd
Attack Damage: 3d crush per round exposed / 4d if player is down
Special Abilities: Width 15m at full spread. Triggers: gunshot (100m auto) / sudden noise 50m (Per roll) / predator smell / supernatural disturbance.
Weakness: Open ground / Climbing above stampede path
Notes: Evasion: Dodge -2. The bay brumby with white blaze: Animal Handling -1 (slightly less reactive). Leads to the platform.

Name: The Marloo
Advantages: Alternate Form (any dead animal in territory) / Doesn't Sleep / Indomitable / Night Vision 9 / Silence 4 / Terrain Adaptation / Unfazeable
Special Abilities: Fright Checks: first sighting -2 / form change observed -5 / circling sounds at night (Will or -1 to all actions)
Weakness: Ochre across threshold (Will -4 to cross) / Running water / Ceremony with correct intent
Notes: Goal: return drill core to gorge. Herds rather than attacks. Withdraws permanently when drill core returned.

Name: Iron Presence
Secondary Attack: Visible at tree line (20-50m) in low light / Disappears within 10m / Never in full daylight. Fright Checks: tree line sighting -2 / footfalls without sight -1 / semi-circular impressions (Will or -1 to all)
Special Abilities: Residue of Robert Cready guilt shaped by Marloo proximity. Fades when Marloo withdraws from gorge.

Name: Stockman's Ghost
Key Skills: Partial transparency / More visible at dusk-dawn / Fades in direct sunlight / Silent
Secondary Damage: Directional information only. Points toward gorge / camp warnings / safe crossings. Fright Check: first appearance 0 / subsequent appearances (familiarity) no check.
Special Abilities: Distance maintained regardless of approach speed
Weakness: Cannot be addressed or detained. Appears in Melbourne bridge scenes pointing toward Spencer Street.

Name: The Drowned Choir
Key Skills: Per roll -2 to resist entering water / Will -2 per round if submerged / Fright Check -3 if submerged
Secondary Damage: After-effect: dreams of the choir for 1d nights following exposure. Swimming against tidal current requires Swimming -2.
Special Abilities: Physical contact from another player breaks effect
Weakness: Not hostile. Draws players deeper into flooded sections. River Dreaming disadvantage on extended exposure.

Name: The Pale Rider
Region: Snowy Mountains — high country tracks at night
Key Skills: Partial visibility / Sound fully present (hooves on granite at full volume) / Passes through player position if not avoided
Primary Attack: 1d crush (if player doesn't move and fails Will -2)
Secondary Damage: Fright Check: -2 first encounter. Cannot be photographed — cameras show empty track. Cannot be chased (Move 28).
Special Abilities: Moving off the track / Will roll success
Weakness: The ride still happening in the mountain. Passes through position — not an attack. Will always attempt to run.

Name: The Mimi
Region: Kimberley — rocky escarpment near ochre gorge
Advantages: Silence 6 / Terrain Adaptation (rock faces/crevices) / Peripheral only (Per -4 to confirm sighting) / Insubstantial / Windstill sensing
Special Abilities: Wind disperses them immediately. Appear only when wind is calm. Communication: Hidden Lore (Dreaming) -2 gives directional information toward gorge.
Weakness: Wind / Direct observation (fades)
Notes: Always present in escarpment. Absent from deep gorge chambers = marker of significant change.

Name: Brolga Dancer
Secondary Damage: Dance pattern readable: Hidden Lore (Dreaming) -1 reveals correct approach to gorge — timing/direction/manner of entry recognised as respectful by Mimi and Marloo.
Weakness: Not supernatural — a bird. But the pattern has been shaped by the country over generations. Equivalent information to Dora Mamid's direct guidance.

Name: Willy Willy
Advantages: Debris strike
Secondary Attack: Significant version (near sites): ST 8 at column edge / DX roll or knockdown within 2m. Direction tracking: Per roll reveals path points toward significant site.
Special Abilities: Always moves toward nearest significant site (thermodynamic / not magical). 1d minutes duration.

Name: Cave Resonance (Embodied)
Region: Nullarbor cave — Karra Cave deepest chamber
Systemic Threat: Will -3 to leave worn circle / Time distortion: 10min game = 1hr real / Will -1 per 10min in circle. Fright Check: -2 if submerged in flooded version. Retrieval: physical touch + name = Will -1 to stand.
Secondary Attack: Not hostile. Produces time distortion and scale recalibration. Permanent effect: Will -1 (enclosed spaces) / Will +1 (open sky at night).

Name: Mountain's Regard (Direct)
Systemic Threat: Cold spot: 3-4C below ambient / Hoofprint contact: Per roll for fragment of the ride experience / Will -1 per 10min sustained sound exposure on alpine meadow / Will -2 hands on stone (30+ sec contact requires Will or cannot release for 1d rounds).
Secondary Attack: Fright Check only if player resists experience (rather than accepting) — then -1. The exchange: horseshoe returned + correct location = mountain's gift delivered. Cannot be fumbled.

Name: The Kurrul
Region: Coastal reef off Cossack / Yarra River (conditions)
Systemic Threat: Bioluminescence (cold blue-green light visible 30m in dark) / Face-Seeking (Per +4 to detect reflected faces at 50m) / Insubstantial
Advantages: No direct attack
Secondary Attack: Fright Checks: light rising -1 / full surfacing -4. Permanent effect: Will -1 on critical fail. Kurrul Dreaming: drowning dreams every night until acknowledged. Counter: +1 Will vs water entities (maximum exposure effect).
Secondary Damage: Not looking into still saltwater at night
Special Abilities: Not hostile. Drawn to reflected human faces. Harm is entirely in seeing the scale accurately. Presence in Yarra during bridge scenes possible.

Name: Entity in the Yarra
Region: Yarra River — basalt channel beneath Spencer Street Bridge
Advantages: No Legs (Aquatic) / Doesn't Breathe / Doesn't Sleep / Indomitable / Unfazeable / Injury Tolerance (Diffuse) / Binding Susceptibility (-6 Move when anchor points intact) / Presence Feeding (Will <12: 1 FP/hr within 50m)
Special Abilities: Fright Checks: displacement sighting -3 (failed Will to look) / margin of failure 1-3: paralysis 1d min + 1d FP / 4-6: River Dreaming disadvantage / 7+: Will -1 permanent.
Weakness: Acknowledgment at Spencer Street (four-part) / Bridge anchor points fully reinforced
Notes: Cannot be fought. Feeding is passive and indifferent. Responds only to acknowledgment — ceases pressure when registered.

Name: Nullarbor Presence
Region: The entire Nullarbor Plain — concentrated in cave system
Key Skills: Environmental mechanics only: Will -1 per 10min exposure on plain (if stationary) / Will -3 in worn circle / time distortion. Fright Check: seeing Kurrul equivalent.  Player who goes outside at night: Will -3. All FP and Will rolls as per main scenario.
Secondary Attack: Physical contact from another player / Dawn
Secondary Damage: Cannot be stat-blocked. Not an entity — the plain breathing. Permanent recalibration: -1 Will enclosed spaces / +1 Will open sky at night. Both effects real and permanent.

Name: The Keeper
Region: Kimberley — track between gorge and station
Advantages: Speaks once then gone
Special Abilities: Country reacts to disrespect in his presence: Marloo activates / temperature drops / equipment fails. Not his action — country's response. Trust: Per roll to notice he knows things he should not. Hidden Lore (Dreaming) -2 for directional gift after correct encounter.
Weakness: Respect / Correct behaviour in presence
Notes: Appears once. Cannot be followed. Gives the one piece the players are missing. Departure: simply not present. Permanent gift: +1 Hidden Lore (Dreaming) to most attentive player.

Name: The Thing That Wears the Drowned
Advantages: Mimic (target loved one — Acting 18) / Detect Emotion (calibrates mimicry in real time) / Cannot cross running water
Primary Attack: No direct attack — draws player to sit with it
Special Abilities: Fright Check -5 on first seeing loved one's face. Trigger: three consecutive Will failures among Last Train passengers. Breaks: another player physical contact + name (Will +2) / player states 'you are not [name]' with conviction (creature Will -2 or retreats).
Weakness: Direct acknowledgment of true nature / Running water / Another player's physical intervention
Notes: Assembles appearance from player's emotional residue during train ride. After-effect: see loved one's face in crowds occasionally for rest of campaign — not the creature, just homesickness.

Name: The Administrative Appetite
Key Skills: Cannot be stat-blocked. Operates through credentials and processes. Attacks: Level 1 (employer contacts 1d days) / Level 2 (professional body complaints 2d weeks) / Level 3 (financial flags 48hr) / Level 4 (administrative death certificates — 48hr window before permanent erasure).
Advantages: Death certificates / FOI reference numbers / Senate committee hearing dates
Special Abilities: Counters: L1 Diplomacy (affected parties) / L2 Law skill or solicitor / L3 cash economy + Information Commissioner (30 days) / L4 matching credential level counter-file.
Weakness: Naming it publicly (publication + Senate testimony) / Custodial testimony pathway
Notes: Not evil. Not conscious. A system with a century of practice. The most dangerous thing in the campaign.

Name: The Last Train Passengers
Systemic Threat: Will rolls per 10 minutes: 1st (no penalty) / 2nd (-1) / 3rd (-2) / 4th+ (-3). Failure table: 1-2 (-1 IQ 10min) / 3-4 (-2 Per rest of journey) / 5 (Will -3 to stand) / 6 (Fright Check -2). Per -2 for direct gaze 5+ seconds.
Secondary Damage: Physical contact from another player / Naming Constance's patient / Reaching Birrarung station
Special Abilities: Not individuals — accumulated residue of hundreds of millions of journeys. Cannot be removed from train while moving. Constance can be moved. Passengers cannot.

Name: Constance Okafor (Passenger State)
Key Skills: Psychology-13 / First Aid-11
Primary Attack: None — she is a person in distress
Secondary Damage: Move is 1: not motivated to move. Marcus (son's name) = key — resets Will to full immediately and she becomes fully present. Psychology roll stabilises her (+2 Will for journey). Name of her patient (Helen) = resolution trigger.
Special Abilities: Physical touch + name of loved one / Hearing son's name
Weakness: Not a threat. Not an enemy. A person who boarded looking for someone dead and stayed because time became confused. Must be recovered — her son is seven.

Name: Curious Local
Key Skills: Current Affairs (local)-15 / Gossip-12 / Carousing-13 / Detect Lies-10 / Streetwise (local)-13
Advantages: Contact Group (entire community skill 12 15+) / Claim to Hospitality
Special Abilities: Whatever players tell them is known to entire town within 24 hours. Roll 1d for what they know (1-2 nothing / 3 recent strangers / 4 name needed / 5 accurate legend / 6 witnessed unexplained event).
Weakness: Diplomacy or Fast-Talk to redirect
Notes: Not malicious. Just interested. The gossip consequence is more dangerous than the person.

Name: Volunteer Searcher
Key Skills: Navigation (Land)-13 / Survival-12 / First Aid-11 / Driving (4WD)-13 / Search-12
Advantages: Contact (local emergency services 9+) / Fit / Claim to Hospitality
Special Abilities: Grid maps show exactly where searchers have been — gaps show where players need to go. Searcher testimony available to investigators.
Weakness: Stealth vs their search pattern / Diplomacy +1 (cooperative)
Notes: Well-intentioned. In the way. Will report unusual activity to SES coordinator. Everything goes in their notes.

Name: Drunk at the Pub
Systemic Threat: Brawling-12 (currently 10) / Carousing-14 / Current Affairs (local legends)-13 / Intimidation-10
Advantages: Alcoholism / Bully / Overconfidence / Stubbornness
Secondary Damage: Information filter: Detect Lies or Psychology -2 to distinguish signal from noise. Roll 1d: 3 (name needed) / 4 (local name for entity) / 5 (location needed) / 6 (event in no record anywhere).
Special Abilities: Intimidation vs Will — backs down on success / Buy him a drink +2 to Reaction
Weakness: Contradicting him = Reaction -2. Dismissing him = confrontation. Half embellishment half accurate legend.

Name: Amateur Paranormal Investigator
Key Skills: Research-13 / Electronics Op (AV)-13 / Current Affairs (paranormal)-14 / Fast-Talk-12 / Photography-13
Advantages: Eidetic Memory (online content) / Contact (paranormal community 12+)
Disadvantages: Curious / Overconfidence / Recklessness / Sense of Duty (audience)
Special Abilities: Fright Check -1 when witnessing event. On success: uploads extraordinary footage immediately. On failure: runs — still uploads from car. Three million views in 48 hours.
Weakness: Fast-Talk or Diplomacy to remove footage / Theft to take device
Notes: Thoroughly researched the location. Thermal camera detects cold spots and temperature differentials. Share research on Diplomacy +1.

Name: Journalist's Editor
Key Skills: Writing-15 / Law (Media/Defamation)-14 / Administration-14 / Research-14 / Diplomacy-15 / Fast-Talk-13 / Psychology-13
Advantages: Contact Group (media industry skill 14 12+) / Legal Enforcement Powers (editorial)
Special Abilities: Publication requires: 2 independent sources per claim (Research roll) + documentary evidence + right of reply sent + legal review complete (1d days). Each legal threat from department: +1d days delay.
Weakness: Provide additional documentation (each piece -1 day delay)
Notes: Not the enemy. The system through which the story exists. Legal department threats are standard procedure.

Name: Craig Holt
Key Skills: Administration-14 / Law (Corporate/Resources)-13 / Fast-Talk-13 / Intimidation-11 / Guns (Pistol)-11
Advantages: Legal Enforcement Powers (corporate) / Wealth (Comfortable) / Contact Group (resources industry skill 13 9+)
Disadvantages: Greed / Cowardice (Will -2 when threatened) / Duty (company 15+) / Secret (no full heritage clearance) / Nightmares
Special Abilities: Confrontation sequence: 1 verbal warning / 2 physical restraint / 3 call backup (20-40min response) / 4 weapon drawn (if player draws first) / 5 lethal force (only if fired upon — aims for legs). Will accepts escape deal if credibly offered immunity.
Weakness: Immunity offer for cooperation / Confrontation at night near gorge (Will 8)
Notes: Has deleted photographs from satellite communicator — recoverable on Electronics Op (Communications)-11. Does not know what is in the drill core. Afraid of it.

Name: Irene (Roadhouse Operator)
Key Skills: Psychology-14 / First Aid-13 / Research-12 / Detect Lies-15 / Current Affairs (Nullarbor)-16
Advantages: Empathy / Common Sense / Contact (emergency services skill 13 12+) / Eidetic Memory (roadhouse)
Special Abilities: Reaction +1 base / +2 if in genuine distress / -1 if dismissive of warning / -3 if ignore warning and return worse. Gives names from triple-zero calls freely. Corkboard requires trust.
Weakness: Respect the warning then ask about it
Notes: Knows one of the returning three personally — will acknowledge if pressed gently but not name them. Not a mystery — a private grief she is entitled to keep.

Name: Sandra Walgett
Key Skills: Anthropology-15 / Archaeology-14 / Research-16 / Law (Heritage/Indigenous)-14 / Diplomacy-14 / Detect Lies-14
Advantages: Cultural Familiarity / Contact (Walgalu community skill 14 9+) / Legal Enforcement Powers (heritage assessor — suspended) / Intuition
Disadvantages: Sense of Duty (great-grandfather's legacy) / Stubbornness / Enemy (Office of Strategic Coordination 9+) / Wealth (Struggling)
Special Abilities: Has memorised full content of 1923 survey including Thomas Walgett's marginal note verbatim. Verification question: 'What did the ochre smell like?' (iron and something sweet). On correct answer: Reaction +4.
Weakness: Tommy Barunga facilitation (Diplomacy +2) / David Carrick contact / Physical visit to Darwin
Notes: Thomas Walgett's great-granddaughter. Went into public service specifically to recover the survey. Cannot be reached by any system-mediated communication.

Name: Private Security Contractor
Key Skills: Guns (Pistol)-14 / Guns (Rifle)-13 / Brawling-13 / First Aid-12 / Driving (4WD)-13 / Intimidation-13 / Law (Trespass/Security)-11 / Observation-13 / Tactics-11
Advantages: Combat Reflexes / Fit / Legal Enforcement Powers (trespass/detention)
Disadvantages: Duty (company 12+) / Code of Honour (no lethal force first) / Greed (negotiable)
Special Abilities: Deployed in pairs / 6-hour rotating shifts / 2km perimeter / 20-40min backup response. Sequence: verbal / restraint / call backup / draw weapon / lethal force (only if fired upon). Lethal force paperwork is catastrophic — they avoid it.
Weakness: Diplomatic cover story (Diplomacy 0) / Knowledge of site significance (causes hesitation — Will roll or steps aside)
Notes: Greed modifier: +2 to negotiations if player offers financial incentive. Code of Honour: will not falsify reports or exceed authority.

Name: Detective (Investigating Players)
Region: All scenarios — when players become visible
Key Skills: Criminology-15 / Detect Lies-15 / Interrogation-14 / Law (Criminal)-14 / Guns (Pistol)-13 / Brawling-12 / Research-14 / Observation-15 / Psychology-13
Advantages: Legal Enforcement Powers / Intuition / Contact Group (police skill 13 12+) / Unfazeable
Disadvantages: Sense of Duty (the law) / Code of Honour (police) / Stubborn
Special Abilities: Detect Lies-15 vs player Fast-Talk or Acting in all encounters. Research roll per day of investigation + Criminology to synthesise pattern. Will not arrest without grounds but notes all inconsistencies and returns.
Weakness: Physical evidence of supernatural that defies framework / Offering them the framework as ally
Notes: Turning point: player who provides undeniable evidence creates choice — file anomaly or acknowledge insufficient framework. Framework-abandoning detective = extremely powerful ally. One of the most dangerous conventional threats.

Name: Mining Site Foreman
Key Skills: Geology-13 / Mechanic (Mining Equipment)-15 / Driving (4WD)-14 / Guns (Rifle)-13 / Survival (Desert)-14 / Leadership-13 / Intimidation-12
Advantages: Fit / Hard to Kill +2 / Danger Sense
Disadvantages: Stubbornness / Duty (company 12+) / Sense of Duty (team) / Superstition (latent)
Special Abilities: Has full equipment logs showing every malfunction/impossible magnetometry reading near gorge. Gives logs on: Diplomacy -1 success OR player demonstrates knowledge of ochre gorge significance (no roll needed). Psychology/Detect Lies to spot performed certainty.
Weakness: Knowledge of gorge contents / Demonstrating respect for the situation
Notes: Not the enemy. Has been waiting for someone to acknowledge what is happening. Checks over shoulder near drill site — habit developed last 6 weeks.

Name: Stockman with a History
Key Skills: Animal Handling-16 / Guns (Rifle)-15 / Brawling-14 / Survival (Desert)-16 / Tracking-15 / Intimidation-14 / Navigation (Land)-15 / Hidden Lore (Dreaming)-12
Advantages: Danger Sense / Combat Reflexes / Fit / Hard to Kill +2
Disadvantages: Stubbornness / Intolerance (country disrespect) / Sense of Duty (country/custodians) / Distrust (strangers initially)
Special Abilities: Reaction starts at -3. Trust building: acknowledge country significance +1 / mention Dora Mamid respectfully +2 / ask permission before entering significant areas +2 / correctly identify Marloo +3. Trust destroyers: company mention positively -2 / bribery attempt -3 (asked to leave).
Weakness: Respect-building sequence (see trust mechanic)
Notes: At Reaction +1 or better: most valuable Kimberley ally. Knows every track/water source/Marloo sighting last 6 months. Knows the resolution. Has been waiting for the right people.

Name: Conspiracy Theorist (Real Information)
Key Skills: Research-17 / Computer Operation-15 / Electronics Op (Communications)-13 / Current Affairs (government/heritage)-15 / Writing-14 / Detect Lies-13
Advantages: Eidetic Memory / Intuition (bureaucratic irregularity)
Disadvantages: Paranoia (mild / with reason) / Curious / Reclusive / Obsession (pattern)[-10]
Special Abilities: Reaches them through: encrypted messaging (3 contact attempts before response) / VPN required / in person only after 5 matching pieces of evidence. Trust: each evidence piece matching their database +1 trust level. 3 pieces = share database. 5 pieces = in-person meeting.
Weakness: Matching evidence pieces from non-public sources
Notes: Has 3-year database of public record evidence for the pattern. Corroborates Margaret's files and adds 3 unknown cases. Personal reason for obsession — family member is one of the unreachable.

Name: Office of Strategic Coordination Field Officer
Region: All scenarios — when players are priority
Key Skills: Administration-18 / Law (all areas)-16 / Psychology-16 / Detect Lies-17 / Diplomacy-16 / Fast-Talk-15 / Intimidation-15 / Research-17 / Computer Operation-16 / Observation-16 / Stealth-13 / Acting-15
Advantages: Legal Enforcement Powers (extensive) / Contact Group (Commonwealth skill 16 15+) / Intuition / Unfazeable / Empathy (as tool)
Special Abilities: Assessment phase: 1d days passive observation in cover role (Acting-15). Cover blown: Per -3 to notice / critical success identifies specific surveillance. Escalation: L1 employer contacts / L2 professional body complaints / L3 financial flags / L4 death certificates.
Weakness: Physical confrontation (they comply completely and call superior within 1 hour — accelerates response)
Notes: Never engage physically. Each instance of physical intimidation adds one escalation level. Most systemically dangerous conventional human in campaign.

Name: Compromised Senior Police Officer
Key Skills: Administration-15 / Law (Criminal)-15 / Intimidation-14 / Leadership-14 / Tactics-13 / Guns (Pistol)-13 / Detect Lies-14 / Politics-13
Advantages: Legal Enforcement Powers (senior police) / Contact Group (police skill 14 15+) / Rank / Claim to Hospitality
Disadvantages: Secret (original favour) [-20] / Duty (police 15+) / Cowardice (re: exposure)[-10] / Stubbornness
Special Abilities: Can: redirect investigations / reassign detective / access warrant databases / flag vehicles / brief department contacts with player locations. Leverage mechanic: if players find original favour (Research -3 or through Sandra Walgett docs): Intimidation/Diplomacy +3 — will not actively help but stops hindering.
Weakness: Original favour evidence / Immunity negotiation pathway (Will -2 to accept / retryable once per session)
Notes: Redemption option: offer exposure on own terms = Will -2 roll. On success: testimony to Senate inquiry. Not irredeemably corrupt — simply protecting one wrong decision with subsequent wrong decisions.

Name: Federal Court Judge (Unnamed)
Special Abilities: Can: issue injunctions / recuse from related cases (correct but causes months delay) / deny Carrick's characterisation of 1997 meeting professionally and plausibly.
Weakness: Name in Carrick's statement is permanent public record — sufficient
Notes: Never met directly. Evidence of system's depth. Present in compendium to establish: when legal channels are attempted, this is the quality of opposition in those channels.

Name: Helen Birch
Key Skills: Administration-19 / Law (all government areas)-17 / Psychology-17 / Detect Lies-18 / Diplomacy-17 / Research-18 / Politics-16 / Acting-15 / Computer Operation-17 / Fast-Talk-16 / Occultism-12
Advantages: Legal Enforcement Powers (maximum Commonwealth level) / Contact Group (entire Commonwealth bureaucracy skill 17 15+) / Intuition / Empathy (genuine) / Eidetic Memory / Rank (departmental secretary equivalent)
Disadvantages: Duty (Office 15+) / Callousness (functional) / Secret (Office existence) / Nightmares (NT spring 2004 / every night 20 years) / Guilt (suppressed — 20pt equivalent)
Special Abilities: Will roll to act against Office: base 16. Modifiers: -1 per scenario visited / -2 for 1923 survey / -3 for Petra Scholl (breaks composure before roll). On failure: unclassifies document / signs Carrick statement / writes own statement. On success: another approach needed.
Weakness: Players being the people who went to the sites and came back / Complete evidence assembly / Petra Scholl's presence
Notes: Not defeated — she decides. The players cannot force it. She was always going to do this eventually. They got there first. She has been to a site (NT spring 2004) and suppressed her own 38-page assessment of it.

Name: Petra Scholl
Key Skills: Geology-14 / Heritage Assessment-16 / Research-15 / Law (Heritage)-13 / Observation-14
Advantages: Hidden Lore (Dreaming)-13 / Intuition
Disadvantages: Enemy (Office of Strategic Coordination 9+) / Wealth (Poor) / Administratively Dead (21 years)
Special Abilities: Has been dead officially for 21 years. Verification for players: hidden documents on Margaret's USB drive (folder OLD-DELETE) with handwritten note 'If you're reading this I was wrong about Darwin.' Handwriting matches 2001 assessment signature.
Weakness: David Carrick's protection / Hidden on USB drive
Notes: The campaign's most important witness. 21 years. Very tired. Wants to be alive again officially and go back to the Pilbara to see if the site still remembers her.

Name: Margaret Osei
Key Skills: Administration-17 / Law (Government/FOI)-15 / Research-16 / Psychology-14 / Detect Lies-15 / Diplomacy-14 / Writing-14
Advantages: Contact Group (Commonwealth bureaucracy 30 years skill 14 15+) / Legal Enforcement Powers (briefly)
Special Abilities: Reaction +2 if approached correctly. Reaction -2 if players are transactional rather than genuine. Gives USB drive in public coffee shop. Knows about David Carrick. Knows about the field officer watching her.
Weakness: Trust built through respectful approach / Public location meeting (protection via witnesses)
Notes: 32 years of notes on the USB drive. Hidden folder contains Petra Scholl's documents. Retirement accelerated — credentials revoked by Monday after players visit. Her fear is real and should be played as real.

Name: David Carrick
Key Skills: Administration-17 / Law (Government)-15 / Research-16 / Psychology-14 / Computer Operation-15
Advantages: Contact Group (former Commonwealth contacts) / Eidetic Memory (the 47 cases)
Disadvantages: Secret (Office involvement 20 years) / Guilt (functional — driving current choice) / Nightmares
Special Abilities: Filed players' death certificates first (before Office could) — creates database conflict buying 48-hour window. Has Petra Scholl in his back room. Was at 1997 meeting where Office's purpose was stated. Will sign document and add his own statement if players have been to the sites.
Weakness: Threat of exposure accelerates cooperation / Petra Scholl's presence reveals full picture
Notes: Not redeemed — making a different choice. Was at the 1997 meeting. Signed off on Petra Scholl's administrative death. Is sorry. That does not make it sufficient. He knows this.

Name: Billy Hanrahan
Key Skills: Animal Handling (Horses)-16 / Survival (Mountain)-17 / Navigation (Land)-15 / Naturalist-15 / Riding (Horse)-16 / Farming-14
Advantages: Danger Sense / Unfazeable / Fit (residual)
Disadvantages: Age (-3 ST/HT already applied) / Loner / Sense of Duty (the mountain)
Special Abilities: Reaction: +2 if approached with respect for his knowledge / -3 if approached with urgency that doesn't acknowledge him first. Intimidation attempts: automatically fail. He is 80. He has been on this mountain 60 years. He is not frightened of people who arrived two days ago.
Weakness: Respectful approach — he will wait as long as needed
Notes: Knows where the platform is. Knows about the trade. Knows the old story before Paterson's poem. Comes and goes without transitional states — on the rock / not on the rock. This is correct.

Name: Drysdale Station Caretaker
Key Skills: Animal Handling-13 / Mechanic (Station Equipment)-12 / Survival (Desert)-14 / Guns (Rifle)-13 / First Aid-11
Primary Attack: None — he is not a threat
Special Abilities: Current condition: Will 9→6 (3 Fright Check failures) / Nightmares / Paranoia (checks mirrors 3x before bed) / Dehydration (-1 HT). Stabilising: Psychology or First Aid -1. Critical success reveals: 'It's not coming for me — it's coming for the drill core in the Aldercroft ute.'
Weakness: Psychology or First Aid to stabilise / Physical needs (food and water)
Notes: Will not leave the homestead. The homestead is survivable. The outside is not. This calculation is not negotiable. Work within it.
